title: General Crisis Intervention Hotlines - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Crisis Hotlines > General Crisis Intervention Hotlines 47 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues At Risk Families Disaster/Emergency Issues Older Adults Transgender Individuals Youth Issues 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES MADRAS MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES JEFFERSON COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 850 SW 4th Street Suite 302 Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 410-6430 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DIVISION CLACKAMAS COUNTY CRISIS AND SUPPORT LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis and support line for mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Services include consultation, evaluation and assistance from mental health professionals. Also facilitates dispatch of the Mobile Crisis Response team. 2051 Kaen Road Suite 154 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 655-8585 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP BEHAVIORAL HEALTHCARE CLATSOP COUNTY 24-HOUR CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County crisis line providing support for people experiencing mental health challenges, thoughts of suicide, and other crises. 115 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 325-5724 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA COMMUNITY MENTAL HEALTH COLUMBIA COMMUNITY MENTAL HEALTH CREEKSIDE CENTER CCMH CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County crisis line providing support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Crisis therapists provide supportive counseling, outreach, assessment, and referral information to individuals experiencing an emotional crisis or interpersonal difficulties. Offers immediate, short-term help to individuals who experience an event that produces emotional, mental, physical, and behavioral distress or problems. 58646 McNulty Way Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 397-5211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS HEPPNER BEHAVIORAL HEALTH COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 550 West Sperry Street Heppner, OR 97836 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing a mental health emergency Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 676-9161 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DESCHUTES COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Crisis services are provided to any individual in need; insurance coverage is not required. Services may include assessment, intervention planning, and information and referral services. Brief crisis stabilization through individual or group treatment may also be provided. 2577 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Walk in: Monday-Friday 8:30am-3:30pm Email (541) 322-7500 9 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH JACKSON COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. Crisis services include risk assessment, safety planning and coordination with community resources and services. In person crisis intervention services are available. 140 S Holly Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm All other serivces: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 774-8201 24 7 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH LINN COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH ALBANY LINN COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Offers initial crisis screening assessment, telephone triage, crisis intervention, pre-commitment investigation, and hospital liaison services. Also offers in-person crisis intervention services during the day. 445 3rd Avenue SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 967-3866 ( 1) Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MARION COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH PSYCHIATRIC CRISIS CENTER MARION COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 1234 Commercial Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 585-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA CENTER FOR LIVING MID-COLUMBIA CENTER FOR LIVING WASCO COUNTY COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 1060 Webber Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 386-7534 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MULTNOMAH COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports people in crisis with mental health issues, suicidal thoughts, or emergencies. Provides phone counseling, guidance on next steps, and can dispatch a mobile team or arrange transport to a care facility. Also offers referrals to mental health and addiction treatment services. Also serves as the front door to Multnomah Behavioral Health Services (mental health and substance use disorder), the behavioral health benefit for Oregon Health Plan members in Multnomah County assigned to Health Share of Oregon CCO. 209 SW 4th Avenue Suite 520 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 988-5887 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC OUTPATIENT PROGRAM BAKER COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 3425 13TH Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 519-7126 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC JOSEPHINE COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support through a county crisis line for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. 1215 SW G Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 474-5360 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTBACK STRONG ADULT SERVICES LAKE COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. Also provides investigations and examinations as they pertain to the civil commitment process. 215 N G Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 947-6021 8 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DALLAS CRISIS HOTLINE AND INTERVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a 24-hour crisis line as well as a range of crisis services including assessment, crisis stabilization, intervention planning, help finding a mental health provider, and information about non-crisis community resources. 182 SW Academy Street Suite 333 Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 623-9289 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH WEST SALEM CRISIS HOTLINE AND INTERVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a 24-hour crisis line as well as a range of crisis services including assessment, crisis stabilization, intervention planning, help finding a mental health provider, and information about non-crisis community resources. 1520 Plaza Street NW Suite 150 Salem, OR 97304 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 623-9289 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SYMMETRY CARE INC HARNEY COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 348 W Adams Street Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Walk-in: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 573-8376 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK FAMILY COUNSELING CENTER TILLAMOOK COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. Service provided for those at-risk of psychiatric hospitalization. 906 Main Avenue Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 842-8201 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WALLOWA VALLEY CENTER FOR WELLNESS CRISIS HOTLINE AND INTERVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 24-hour crisis line offers a range of crisis services and interventions including crisis screening, pre-commitment evaluations, alternatives to hospitalization, short-term therapeutic interventions, and crisis stabilization. 606 Medical Parkway Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 398-1175 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH YAMHILL COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 627 NE Evans Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Walk in: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm (503) 434-7523 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AGRISAFE NETWORK AGRISTRESS HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line. Services offered include suicide assessment, emotional support, and referrals to resources. 136 Terra Bella Boulevard Covington, LA 70433 Eligibility: Individuals who are employed in agricultural, fishery, or forestry industries Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 897-2474 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CALL BLACKLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a hotline focusing on Black, Indigenous, People of Color, and LGBTQ2+ individuals who have experienced harmful, physical, and inappropriate contact with police and vigilantes. Provides an anonymous and confidential avenue to report interactions and referrals to resources. Volunteers are trained to listen and affirm the experiences of anyone who may have experienced a negative interaction with law enforcement, consumer service (restaurants and stores), or vigilante contact. Volunteer opportunities are available. Confidential Mailing Address Eligibility: Unrestricted; prioritizes BIPOC (Black, Indigenous, and People of Color) and LGBTQ2+ individuals Hours: Monday-Friday 6am-2pm, Saturday-Sunday 4pm-8pm Email (800) 604-5841 Visit Website More Details CANYON CRISIS AND RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, information and referral, support groups, outreach services, counseling, and advocacy. Help with filling out restraining orders and victim compensation papers may be offered. Also provides essential items such as blankets, clothing, shampoo, and deodorant. 825 NW Santiam Boulevard Mill City, OR 97360 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 897-2327 24- Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CRISIS HOTLINE AND INTERVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Crisis line offers a range of crisis services and interventions including screening assessment, triage and intervention. 2301 Cove Avenue La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 962-8800 6 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COOS HEALTH AND WELLNESS COOS COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. A mobile response team can be activated by contacting the crisis line. 281 LaClair Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 266-6800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CRISIS TEXT LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides crisis support via text message, WhatsApp, and webchat. Texters are connected with live, trained crisis counselors. The Crisis Text Line is anonymous, and any information shared with a counselor remains confidential. Volunteer opportunities are available. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (443) 787-6778 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELP NOW! ADVOCACY CRISIS RESOLUTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides crisis resolution through giving strategic advice and negotiating to resolve a dispute or obtain items or services for the client at no cost or reduced cost. Also assists with information and resources. 3441 Blackpine Drive Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 732-1911 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH LANE COUNTY-WIDE CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a general crisis line. 2411 Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 682-1001 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWAYS INC TREASURE VALLEY REGION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 702 Sunset Drive Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 823-9050 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINCOLN COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH LINCOLN COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. Crisis and triage services include follow-up care after a crisis and brief therapy, after-hours counselor contact, hospitalization for acute mental health issues, and civil commitments from court proceeding for those who cannot care for their needs or are a danger to themselves or others. 51 SW Lee Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (866) 266-0288 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINES FOR LIFE YOUTHLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis intervention line. Additionally provides support and referrals around such topics as dealing with bad test grades, running away from home, homelessness, a recent breakup, depression, anxiety, bullying, pregnancy, self-harm, or substance use disorders. 5100 SW Macadam Avenue Suite 400 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Youth ages 10-24 and their families Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (877) 968-8491 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINES FOR LIFE SENIOR LONELINESS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing loneliness, isolation, depression, or anxiety. Provides active listening, emotional and grief support, information and referrals, and suicide and crisis intervention. 5100 SW Macadam Avenue Suite 400 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Age 55 and older Hours: 7 days per week 5:30am-11:30pm Email (800) 282-7035 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES STATION 7 STATION 7 SHELTER AND CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth crisis intervention services and a temporary youth shelter. Services include immediate crisis intervention and counseling, youth and family assessments, and referral assistance. Shelter services offer short-term overnight stays, group counseling, and individual case management. Individuals in crisis may also use the shelter for temporary respite while a long-term plan is developed. 931 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: -Runaway and homeless youth ages 11-20 -Youth and families in conflict Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 689-3111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MARION COUNTY CHILDREN'S BEHAVIORAL HEALTH YOUTH & FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Children's Behavioral Health Services work with children presenting with problems of behavior, anxiety, depression, psychotic thinking, and trauma related issues. Services include individual, family, and group therapy that focuses on strategic and structural family issues and support, trauma-related care, and wraparound services (for high needs children). 3867 Wolverine Street NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Ages 0-18 Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 8:30am-7pm Wednesday/Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 588-5352 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MARION COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH PSYCHIATRIC CRISIS CENTER BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CRISIS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Behavioral Health Crisis Center (BHCC) provides in-person crisis intervention. Services range from stabilization, mental health screening, hospitalization, respite and referrals for on-going case management, as well as short-term emergency prescriptions. At least one Qualified Mental Health Professional (QMHP) is on duty at all times to provide face-to-face assessments and crisis counseling. 1234 Commercial Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Adults experiencing a mental health crisis Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 585-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MARION COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH WOODBURN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CRISIS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Behavioral Health Crisis Center (BHCC) provides in-person crisis intervention. Services range from stabilization, mental health screening, hospitalization, respite and referrals for on-going case management, as well as short-term emergency prescriptions. At least one Qualified Mental Health Professional (QMHP) is on duty at all times to provide face-to-face assessments and crisis counseling. 976 N Pacific Highway Woodburn, OR 97071 Eligibility: Adults experiencing a mental health crisis Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 585-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON CHINESE COALITION OCC HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a support line for Chinese community members in crisis situations, including being a victim of a crime, being lost in the streets, receiving an eviction notice, experiencing depression and needing to talk with someone, or needing interpretation services. The support line is for urgent situations only. In most cases, the support will be provided remotely. 219 NW 3rd Avenue #227 Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Chinese community members in crisis or urgent situations Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week, though service hours may vary based on volunteer availability Email (503) 334-1010 , Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MONMOUTH CRISIS HOTLINE AND INTERVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a 24-hour crisis line as well as a range of crisis services including assessment, crisis stabilization, intervention planning, help finding a mental health provider, and information about non-crisis community resources. 1310 Main Street E Monmouth, OR 97361 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 623-9289 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIVERVIEW CENTER FOR GROWTH MOBILE RESPONSE AND STABILIZATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support to children experiencing a mental health crisis. Program crisis workers can provide assistance and referrals to help stabilize the situation, and create a short-term plan. On-site services available for more intensive intervention needs. The team may seek authorization for a brief, voluntary crisis care placement, approved by the parent. 3995 Marcola Road Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Families with youth age 20 and younger who are experiencing a mental health crisis Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (888) 989-9990 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES SIUSLAW AREA RESOURCE CENTER DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource, referral, and crisis line that primarily focuses on survivors of domestic/sexual violence, and human trafficking and stalking. In addition to providing a crisis line, offers support in the form of a domestic violence safe house, protection orders, advocacy, and support groups. 1536 W 12th Street Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Primarily focuses on survivors of domestic/sexual violence; other emergency calls are accepted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 997-4444 24- Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANS LIFELINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a volunteer-run hotline that offers crisis support and connection to resources where possible such as passport and state identification help to reflect gender identity. While the goal is to prevent self-harm, it is also a warmline where people can call with a variety of needs (i.e., how to access transition-related health care, where to find local support groups). The hotline is staffed by trans-identifying individuals. Operators will only call emergency services with the caller's consent. 548 Market Street Pmb 15337 San Francisco, CA 941045401 Eligibility: Any trans-identifying person and anyone who may be questioning their gender identity Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm; closed some holidays Email (877) 565-8860 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRAUMA INTERVENTION PROGRAM NW Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Trained and screened volunteers provide emotional and practical support to people involved in traumatic events and their families in the first few hours following a tragedy: complete follow-up calls, information, and referral to other resources (burial services, probate information, hazardous clean up, and other services). Volunteers are requested to crisis scenes by police officers, firefighters, hospital personnel, organizations/businesses, and schools after a traumatic event. Eligibility: People involved in traumatic (i.e., an unexpected death) or crisis events and their families Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 940-7997 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES SUBSTANCE ABUSE AND MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES ADMINISTRATION 988 SUICIDE AND CRISIS LIFELINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a national network of local crisis centers that offer free and confidential emotional support to people in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. 5600 Fishers Lane Rockville, MD 20857 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 273-8255 988 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH HILLSBORO RECOVERY CENTER WASHINGTON COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Staffed with master's-level clinicians who are trained to provide telephone crisis counseling. Also offers referrals for follow-up routine treatment for ongoing support and services, even if the caller is not eligible for county-funded services. If a crisis cannot be resolved over the phone, the crisis line may direct the caller to a local emergency room or dispatch the Washington County Mobile Crisis Team to come to the caller. 5250 NE Elam Young Parkway Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 291-9111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WHITE BIRD CLINIC CRISIS COUNSELING HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers over-the-phone crisis counseling and provides information and referral for other local resources. 323 E 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Individuals in crisis Hours: 7 days per week 9am-5pm Email (541) 687-4000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH FAMILY & YOUTH SERVICES YAMHILL COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 420 NE 5th Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Walk in: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm (503) 434-7462 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL
